data.title: Etteplan strengthens its global footprint in fiber routing solutions as demand for high-density optical fiber flex circuits grows
data.description: In April 7, 2026, in Espoo, Finland, the demand for Etteplan's cutting-edge fiber routing and coating solutions is surging worldwide. Industries are turning to high-density optical fiber flex circuits for advanced applications, leading to increased interest in Etteplan's offerings. These optical fiber flex circuits are crucial for various sectors due to their versatility and reliability.

data.title: BTC stalls below $70K amid profit-taking, reaching new April highs. Trump increases pressure on Iran.
data.description: Bitcoin encountered significant resistance upon reaching the $70,000 threshold, resulting in new highs for April. Analysts attribute this to profit-taking pressure in the market.

data.title: HD Hyundai Avikus Spearheads Global Standards of Autonomous Navigation Support
data.description: The initial universal autonomous navigation support system to receive Technical Approval (TA) is set to accelerate commercialization, following validation by DNV over a three-year period. This system is anticipated to play a crucial role in shaping the International Maritime Organization's (IMO) standards for autonomous navigation. HD Hyundai is poised to drive forward the commercialization of autonomous navigation technology, leveraging this pivotal development.
France’s Brégeon cautions that gas bills will probably climb.

data.title: Share buyback programme – week 14
data.description: The Nasdaq Copenhagen, Euronext Dublin, and London Stock Exchange have joined with the Danish Financial Supervisory Authority and other key stakeholders.

data.title: Renk Group AG: Release according to Article 40 (1) of the WpHG (the German Securities Trading Act) with the objective of Europe-wide distribution
data.description: Renk Group AG has issued a statement in accordance with Article 40 (1) of the German Securities Trading Act (WpHG), intending to distribute it across Europe. The company's announcement aims to reach a wide audience and comply with regulatory requirements.

data.title: Hunting PLC (“Hunting” or “the Company” or “the Group”) $63.5 Million of Subsea Orders for Guyana
data.description: In London, Hunting PLC (LSE: HTG) has announced securing orders amounting to $63.5 million for its titanium stress joint (“TSJ”) product line, related to a fresh offshore project in Guyana. The orders will be fulfilled by the Subsea Spring business unit of the company and are scheduled for completion by May 2028. Revenue recognition is set to commence from the second half of 2026. Additionally, this fresh TSJ order supplements the $4.4 million in orders previously procured by the Group’s Stafford division.

data.title: Announcement of the net asset value of INVL Technology as of 31 December 2025
data.description: Based on Section XI of the Articles of Association of INVL Technology, the Company's net asset value as of December 31, 2025, stood at EUR 64,624,072, equivalent to EUR 5.4013 per share.

data.title: Audited results of INVL Technology of 2025
data.description: As of December 31, 2025, INVL Technology reported equity and net asset value totaling EUR 64.6 million, marking a 26% increase from the previous year. The per share value for equity and NAV stood at EUR 5.4013.

data.title: INVL Technology's presentation showcases a total investment of €10 million in innovative tech solutions.
data.description: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has reached an all-time high of 10.2%. This translates to over 15 million people currently out of work. The report also stated that the GDP has seen a significant decrease of 5% in the last quarter. Additionally, the government announced a stimulus package of $2 trillion in response to the economic downturn. The stock market saw a sharp decline, with the main index dropping by 8% in just one week. Experts predict that it may take several years for the economy to fully recover from these unprecedented challenges.
